preamble: The complete loss or elision of the voiceless bilabial plosive /p/, a characteristic sound change in the history of Celtic languages and various other lineages.

examples.note: Latvian dropped word-final stops including *-p. Final consonant loss is a distinctive Latvian innovation compared to conservative Lithuanian which often retains final consonants.

examples.to: mempergi [but base p deleted in most stems]
examples.note: Indonesian meN- prefix: initial /p/ is deleted in many bases, with the nasal assimilating to [m]. E.g., 'me- + pukul' → 'memukul' (to hit), underlying /p/ deleted.
Complete loss of PIE *p in Old Irish.

sources: Lewis, H., & Pedersen, H. (1937). A Concise Comparative Celtic Grammar.
